title: Indulgent Portuguese Pecan Fried-Rice
description: A fresh, crispy midnight craving Portuguese dish featuring pecan nuts, a lacquer-glaze, and dairy-free-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add diced onion and minced garlic, sauté until onion becomes translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in diced bell pepper and smoked paprika, cook until peppers soften, about 4 minutes.
Add cooked rice to the skillet, breaking up clumps with a spatula, and cook until heated through, about 5 minutes.
In a small bowl whisk together soy sauce, maple syrup, and lemon juice to create a glaze.
Pour the glaze over the rice mixture, stirring constantly until the rice is evenly coated and glistens, about 2 minutes.
Fold in the chopped pecans, allowing them to toast lightly, for 1 minute.
Season the dish with salt and pepper to taste, adjusting the glaze if needed.
Remove from heat and transfer to a serving dish.
Serve immediately while hot, enjoying the sweet, salty, and nutty flavor profile.
